channel.title: Algorithms on RadixArk Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Algorithms on RadixArk Blog

item.title: Quantum Error Correction Algorithms
item.description:
Quantum states decay. An uncorrected qubit will inevitably interact with the environment, leading to decoherence and bit-flip or phase-flip errors.

The Problem of Decoherence

If a qubit is naturally in state \( |\psi\rangle = \alpha|0\rangle + \beta|1\rangle \), environmental interaction introduces a depolarizing channel. The fidelity of the state drops exponentially over time:

$$ F(t) = e^{-\frac{t}{T_2}} $$

Shor’s Error Correction Code

By encoding a single logical qubit into nine physical qubits, we can project errors onto a syndrome without measuring (and thus collapsing) the logical state itself. The parity check matrix \( H \) must satisfy:
